feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: How do we feel about it
entry.content: How do we feel about it

entry.title: /mu/ Music: At least Chan Marshall's not trying to fool anybody. From "she plays the difficu…
entry.content: At least Chan Marshall's not trying to fool anybody. From "she plays the difficult parts and I play difficult" to "the music is boring me to death," she's an honest heroine of the new indie staple--not noise-tune and certainly not irony, both as passé as the guilty pop dreams they kept at bay, but sadness. Slow sadness. Slow sadness about one's inability to relate. And not to audiences. Hell is other people. C+

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: i had a very interesting 3 pronged dream the first part of the dream was that i …
entry.content: i had a very interesting 3 pronged dream the first part of the dream was that i was back in college and met all these interesting irl friends i used to have 15-20 years ago. also for some reason in the dream everyone has two cell phones - a smartphone for using the internet, and a flip phone for making phone calls. it doesn't really make any sense but that's just how this dreamspace worked the second part of the dream was i went to anime club. i didn't pay any attention to the anime at all cause

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Controlling language
entry.content: Why are we allowing tech bros to do 1984 tier fuckery of our language? ”We need moar compute!" No one has ever said this. You never had a fast computer and said ’Wow, this has a lot of compute!'. You'd say it had a lot of processing power, or maybe computational power. You'd never say compute. These AI evangelist are pretty much a cult at this point and it's insane. As much as I want AI to fail and think it could fail, I think it won't. The whole endgame of the surveillance economy is that they

entry.title: /int/ International: Why is the British media recycling this same story each week?
entry.content: Every week there's a new news story in the UK like this. The British media, for some reason, really wants you to believe that people routinely fall into waist-deep, still water, refuse to stand up, and against self-preservation instincts, casually let themselves drown. Is this an attempt at getting people desensitised to even less plausible news stories in the future?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Elliot Page Is Actually Playing Sinon In The Odyssey
entry.content: It turns out, after the Right-wingers like Elon Musk absolutely freaked out under the guide of "historical accuracy" following fan theories that Elliot Page would play Achilles (a mythical person, by the way) in Christopher Nolan's adaptation of The Odyssey, he’s actually be playing someone else. Elliot will star as Sinon, Odysseus's cousin, who convinces the Trojans to let in a giant wooden horse left by the Greek army into the city of Troy, which actually serves as a vessel for the soldiers to

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Is this reasonably enough security for anons to chat with?
entry.content: It is a custom or forked headscale coordination server that disallows UDP to force DERP relays. DERP relays can be self hosted or set to specific trusted ones, alternatively tailscale offers free public ones. The downside for the DERPS, may be that Anons dont want tailscale (the company) or alternatively, the custom or self hosted DERPs to see their IPs. In either choice, the raspi devices can be set to force TOR routing as a way of layering TOR between anons and the DERPs, as well as can be lay

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: A debunking of a British news article that came out today.
entry.content: Every week there's a new news story in the UK like this. The British media wants you to believe that people routinely fall into waist-deep, still water, refuse to stand up, and let themselves drown. Why? Is this the British media's attempt at desensitising people for future, even less-plausible news stories?

Then when the jew lawyers come after me, I'd use my billions to buy a private island, declare my island to be an independent nation and then say I don't live in their jurisdiction, therefore they can't sue me.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Zoomers obsession with "so bad it's good" content
entry.content: A daily reminder that "so bad it's good" culture propagated by companies like Vinegar Syndrome, RLM "Best of the Worst", Sam Hyde, Doug Walker, and MST3K is terrible for the soul and one of the tactics they use to demoralize a population. Whether you participate in Option A or Option B, you are pure scum: Option A >"Don't you get it? Buying VINEGAR SYNDROME MOVIES is being part of an INSIDE JOKE!!!!" >"LOl look at the trailer and all the awkward guttural laugh moments in the movie. How incompete

entry.title: /int/ International: Fatties of /int/ You ARE going to lose weight in the summer, right? I'm going to…
entry.content: Fatties of /int/ You ARE going to lose weight in the summer, right? I'm going to do it with swimming and when summer ends I will continue maintaining with jogging. You have no fucking excuse. Swimming doesn't penalize overweight like jogging does. You can rapidly lose weight this way. It's perfect for fatties. I have July and August to become thin. LET'S FUCKING GO. Swimming 5-6 days a week unless there is thunder or some shit.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Help me
entry.content: My ex-girlfriend cheated on me with a generic local club promoter and told me my music is absolute trash and I will never amount to anything. I spent the last 3 months locked in my room working on this single track just to prove that bitch wrong. I want to make this viral so she sees it everywhere. Please bump and share this if you want to help a fellow anon ruin his ex's mood. Is it actually bad? Honest thoughts?

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Right-wing Americans dream of Europe becoming one 'white' mass with no distincti…
entry.content: Right-wing Americans dream of Europe becoming one 'white' mass with no distinctions between nation-states. They want it to become like America, which is, every european should have some blood of a German, a little bit French, a little bit Finnish, Bulgarian, Serbian, Anglo-Saxon, Italian, Portuguese, and Russian. For them the idea of nationalism seems to be unthinkable. For them, only the idea of 'racionalism' exists.

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: For all the newfags: You're only supposed to check dubs if the person first call…
entry.content: For all the newfags: You're only supposed to check dubs if the person first calls them in advance and tells you to check them. Dubs are not rare. Roughly every 10th post is going to have dubs. It only becomes somewhat noteworthy if the poster correctly calls their repeating digits in advance. Otherwise, you're just pointlessly noting the naturally occurring repeating digits that happen all the time. I see a lot of people improperly checking dubs that were not called in advance, which shows that

entry.title: /o/ Auto: New Car Tech Just Dropped
entry.content: >all new American vehicles will have full 360 cameras inside and outside the car >cameras will do facial and retinal scan of all those inside and outside the car and compile the data in a national record and cross reference it with a criminal database >steering wheels and dashboards will have fingerprint censors Basically it's a good thing because it can protect you from car thieves. If somebody tries to break into your car, their biometrics will be identified. If your car is successfully stolen

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Cream Thread
entry.content: Why is Cream so underrated these days? I know Clapton's covid comments made him a pariah amongst Rolling Stone writers (a big worshiper of Clapton/God) but even still, they removed Cream's albums from the greatest of all time list. Not that many Spotify listeners (same with Jimi Hendrix). I started playing guitar in 2009 after hearing Clapton on the Steppin Out from the Live Cream II album. I couldn't tell you then why I liked Cream so much then, but listening back now, after getting into Jazz m
